hints.text: Bollards **round, baton-like**: rectangular front reflector + two circular back reflectors (white or orange)
Least consistent Baltic poles: round wooden (± diagonal supports) + square concrete (LT-like); trident tops; wooden poles w/ **Nordic metal caps** (also SE/FI/NO, sometimes LV)
Outer lines sometimes **short dashes** (also LT; Sweden mostly)

Chevrons **red w/ white arrows** (like RU/UA; LV/LT inverted!); Baltic km markers: Estonia's **perpendicular to the road, numbers both sides**; warning-sign border thin red **to the edge** (no white outline); **guardrails without reflectors** (LV: white/red, LT: orange)
Pedestrian signs 3 stripes + **detailed person (shoes, attached head)** (LT: simpler, LV: 5 stripes); bus-stop sign 4-window bus (LV: 5); **blue-white signpost wrappings** (also FI/SE)
**Estonian** (Finnic!): Ä Ö Ü + **Õ**; street = **tänav (tn)** / road = **tee** — unique words (Finnish -tie looks similar); many streets lack a suffix entirely
**Oval blue house numbers** (not LV/LT; also Russia)
Wood + white brick, steep corrugated metal roofs; **most wooden buildings of the Baltics**
Directional signs blue/white w/ white outline (FI builds the arrow into the border; LV arrows more stylised)
Gen3 often has a **'green blur'** tint on part of the panorama (not LV/LT; also Gotland/Sweden); Gen3 covers many **very rural roads** — thin rural Gen3 in a Baltic round leans Estonia
hints.uniq_note: The green blur is the Estonia tell within the Baltics — Sweden's Gotland shares it, but the language separates instantly.

shared_with.note: The island of Gotland has the same green-tinted Gen3 blur.
shared_with.diff: Estonian language + Baltic context → Estonia; Swedish + Gotland's white sand/limestone houses → Sweden.

hints.text: Fairly flat (max 318 m) — hilliest: southeast + southern Lääne-Viru; **Russian bilingual signs** → Ida-Viru (Narva area)
Old town = perfectly preserved **medieval/renaissance** area (Central-Europe look) — unlike anywhere else in Estonia
Island: all **Gen4 late-autumn** (brown leaves) + moody dark near-sunset clouds (Tartu has some autumn too)
